final_answer:
Yes, there are real differences between how BSC works and how your current Gold Chain validator admission works.

Why it matters: if your target is “BSC-like behavior,” your current validator onboarding flow is materially different in election model, admission path, and economic thresholds, so launch assumptions and decentralization behavior will differ.

BSC (official docs):
- Validator creation is done through native BSC staking flow (`Become a Validator` / `createValidator`) with operator + consensus + vote key setup and BLS proof.
- The docs state validator creation requires >2001 BNB available (lock + self-delegation).
- Validator selection is staking-ranking based: daily top-45 active set, then 21 consensus validators per epoch (18 from cabinets, 3 from candidates).

Your current Gold Chain (from your current codebase):
- Admission is bridge/event-driven and finalized only after validator-side approval threshold (>2/3 voting power).
- Your configured economic/slot defaults are much lower and tighter (small minimums, small validator-cap default), which is not BSC-equivalent out of the box.

Next steps:
1. Choose explicitly: keep current bridge-confirmed admission model, or move to BSC-style native staking election flow.
2. If BSC-like is required, align thresholds/election logic/key model first, then remove non-BSC admission gates.

Sources:
- https://docs.bnbchain.org/bnb-smart-chain/validator/overview/
- https://docs.bnbchain.org/bc-fusion/validators/creation/
- https://docs.bnbchain.org/bnb-smart-chain/validator/manage-keys/
- https://docs.bnbchain.org/bnb-smart-chain/staking/developer-guide/
- https://github.com/bnb-chain/bsc
